interest: The shared interest of the exhausted teaching profession, planning families, the digital-economy/STEM sector, and evidence-first policy actors in ensuring the audit produces a real decision point rather than cover for continued inaction.
value: Procedural integrity / accountability versus the political convenience of open-ended deferral (credible commitment vs. flexibility).
fear: That 'reform is coming, maybe' becomes a permanent limbo — the profession keeps absorbing announcements without funding, families cannot plan multi-year gimnazium commitments, and another 1-2 cohorts pass through an unexamined system while nothing is actually decided.
assumption: That a Hungarian statutory deadline can in practice be made non-extendable and enforced — i.e., that legal binding actually forecloses the path-of-least-resistance deferral rather than being circumvented by escape clauses or at-will extension.
empirical_uncertainty: Contested-to-unknown: whether a statutory deadline can actually prevent deferral is untested in Hungary and flagged as an open uncertainty in the scenario itself, so the enforceability question is not factually settled.
